name: Sanity Meter
description: A psychological pressure resource that changes as the player experiences darkness, threats, isolation, scripted events, or recovery sources.
design_notes: A sanity meter works best when it communicates vulnerability without becoming a hidden punishment bar. Designers should define decay sources, recovery sources, thresholds, hallucination triggers, save behavior, and how much of the meter is visible. The system should support tension pacing while keeping cause and recovery legible.
design_purpose: Add a long-form fear pressure that survives beyond a single chase or scare.
Tie darkness, monsters, safe rooms, and resource decisions into one readable survival loop.
Trigger hallucinations, audio changes, UI distortions, or encounter pressure at authored thresholds.
player_fantasy: The player feels their character fraying under pressure and looks for light, shelter, or progress before control slips further.

parameters.name: fear_state_id
parameters.description: Named state derived from current sanity thresholds.

parameters.name: sanity_points
parameters.description: Current psychological stability measured for thresholds and effects.

parameters.name: sanity_decay_rate
parameters.description: Drain profile applied by darkness, pursuit, isolation, or scripted pressure.

parameters.name: sanity_recovery_source_id
parameters.description: Recovery source that restores or stabilizes sanity.

parameters.name: threshold_effect_id
parameters.description: Effect triggered when sanity crosses a defined band.

parameters.name: checkpoint_restore_state
parameters.description: Restore rule applied after reload or checkpoint restart.

edge_cases: Sanity drops below a critical threshold during a cutscene, menu, or non-interactive scare.
A recovery source and decay source overlap inside a safe room doorway or light boundary.
A hallucination spawns during combat and is mistaken for a real damage source.
Save/load restores sanity but not the currently active threshold effect.
Accessibility intensity settings disable some effects while the underlying sanity state still changes.
common_bugs: Sanity decay continues while paused because it reads the wrong clock.
Threshold effects retrigger every frame when sanity hovers near a boundary.
Recovery applies client-side and desyncs the authoritative run or checkpoint state.
Visual distortion remains active after fear_state_id returns to a calmer band.
The UI shows rounded sanity values that disagree with threshold behavior.
balancing_notes: Sanity pressure should give players enough warning to react before severe effects begin.
Recovery sources need scarcity, placement, and readable tradeoffs so safe rooms do not erase all tension.
Hidden sanity can increase fear, but invisible cause-and-effect can feel arbitrary.
accessibility_notes: Offer reduced distortion, reduced camera movement, and lower-intensity hallucination settings.
Provide subtitle or text alternatives for audio-only sanity cues.
Let players choose whether the sanity state is numeric, banded, hidden, or simplified.

implementation_notes.unity: Track sanity_points, equivalent to a sanity_value field in some codebases, and fear_state_id in a gameplay state component. Drive post-processing, audio snapshots, and UI from threshold changes rather than per-frame polling, then persist checkpoint_restore_state and active threshold_effect_id so reloads clear or restore effects intentionally.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an autoload fear state manager or Resource to own sanity bands, decay sources, and recovery sources. Emit state_changed signals for Control UI, audio buses, and shader effects, and debounce threshold crossings with hysteresis.
implementation_notes.unreal: A component or Gameplay Attribute can hold sanity_value while Gameplay Tags represent fear_state_id. Authority should resolve recovery and decay events, then replicate band changes and cue ids to clients for camera, sound, and UI presentation.
implementation_notes.web: Keep sanity changes in a fixed-step gameplay reducer and render effects from derived fear_state_id. Persist sanity, threshold effect ids, and accessibility intensity settings together so browser refreshes do not leave visual effects stuck.

example_games.title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
example_games.note: Uses mental-state pressure connected to darkness and frightening events.

example_games.title: Eternal Darkness: Sanity's Requiem
example_games.note: Uses sanity effects as part of its horror presentation.
